titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Religious Education Association: an Association of Professors, Practitioners, and Researchers in Religious Education records
Religious Education Association: an Association of Professors, Practitioners, and Researchers in Religious Education records
titlestmt.author: Martha Lund Smalley

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday July 22, 2025 at 18:00

did.unittitle: Religious Education Association: an Association of Professors, Practitioners, and Researchers in Religious Education records
did.abstract: These are the official archives of the Religious Education Association: an Association of Professors, Practitioners, and Researchers in Religious Education, an organization that was formed in 2003 by the merger of the Religious Education Associatino and the Association of Professors and Researchers in Religious Education. The REA:APPRRE is the world's oldest and largest association of scholars and researchers in the field of religious education.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Religious Education Association was founded in 1903 by William Rainey Harper. In 1970 the Association of Professors and Researchers in Religious Education (APRRE) was born from an earlier section of the National Council of Churches. In 2003 REA and APRRE joined to form REA: an Association of Professors, Practitioners, and Researchers in Religious Education. For more historical information see the website of the REA.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These records of the Religious Education Association: an Association of Professors, Practitioners, and Researchers in Religious Education document the continuation of the ecumenical work of its predecessor organizations, the Religious Education Association and the Association of Professor and Researchers in Religious Education, which merged in 2003. Records of the predecessor organizations are found in Record Groups 74, 74A, 154, and 154A.
Through its annual meetings, newsletter, journal, and website the REA:APPRRE has sought to coordinate and encourage work in the field of religious education.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are organized in six series: I: Organization and Policy Records; II: Board and Committee Records; III: Secretarial Files; IV: Meetings; V: Publications; VI: Financial Records.

corpname.part: Association of Professors and Researchers in Religious Education
